gaps.snippet: impl < V > Sender < V > { # [doc = " Non-blocking send. Returns `TrySend::Sent` on success, `TrySend::Full(v)` if the"] # [doc = " buffer is at capacity, or `TrySend::Closed(v)` if the channel is closed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The value is **always returned on failure** — never dropped silently (G2 / ADR-020 §4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: **Exact** (FIFO push into `VecDeque`; deterministic given the buffer state)."] pub fn try_send (& self , value : V) -> TrySend < V > { let mut inner = self . inner . lock () . unwrap () ; if inner . closed { return TrySend :: Closed (value) ; } if inner . buf . len () >= inner . capacity { return TrySend :: Full (value) ; } inner . buf . push_back (value) ; TrySend :: Sent } # [doc = " Close the channel from the sender side."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " After this, `try_recv` on a drained buffer returns `TryRecv::Closed`. gaps.snippet: impl < V > Receiver < V > { # [doc = " Non-blocking receive. Returns `TryRecv::Received(v)` if a message is buffered,"] # [doc = " `TryRecv::Empty` if the buffer is empty and the channel is still open, or"] # [doc = " `TryRecv::Closed` if the channel is closed and the buffer is drained."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the **normal drain path** — values are delivered to the receiver, no"] # [doc = " reclamation is needed. For the teardown path (values in transit that will never be"] # [doc = " delivered), use [`Receiver::close_with_reclaim`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: **Exact** (FIFO pop from `VecDeque`; deterministic given the buffer state)."] pub fn try_recv (& self) -> TryRecv < V > { let mut inner = self . inner . lock () . unwrap () ; if let Some (v) = inner . buf . pop_front () { return TryRecv :: Received (v) ; } if inner . closed { TryRecv :: Closed } else { TryRecv :: Empty } } # [doc = " The canonical channel identity shared by this `Receiver` and its paired `Sender`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Guarantee: **Exact** — allocated once at channel construction; the same id is returned"] # [doc = " by both the `Sender` and `Receiver` ends."] # [must_use] pub fn channel_id (& self) -> ChannelNodeId { self . inner . lock () . unwrap () . channel_id } # [doc = " Teardown path: drain all buffered values, emit one `ReclamationRecord(ChannelClose)`"] # [doc = " per value through `sink`, then mark the channel closed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the **third live reclamation trigger** (RFC-0027 §9 / §7.3 / G2): when a"] # [doc = " receiver disconnects while values are still in transit (sent but never to be received),"] # [doc = " those values' ownership is released and MUST be reclaimed — one record per value, never"] # [doc = " silently dropped (G2 / never-silent principle)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " ## Distinction from normal drain"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - **Normal drain (`try_recv`):** values are delivered to the receiver (no loss, no"] # [doc = "   reclamation needed). The receiver is the intended consumer; ownership transfers normally."] # [doc = " - **`close_with_reclaim` (this method):** the receiver is tearing down and will NEVER"] # [doc = "   deliver the buffered values. The values are undeliverable in-transit; this method"] # [doc = "   releases their ownership and emits a `ChannelClose` reclamation record for each."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `hash_of` closure supplies the `value_meta_hash` for each value (RFC-0027 §9 field)"] # [doc = " because `V` is an arbitrary type with no inherent [`ContentHash`] (KISS — never"] # [doc = " over-constrain `V`). The channel is marked closed after"] # [doc = " the drain. No value in the buffer at call time can escape without emitting a record."] pub fn close_with_reclaim (self , sink : & mut dyn ReclamationSink , scope_id : ScopeId , sweep_epoch : SweepEpoch , hash_of : impl Fn (& V) -> ContentHash ,) -> usize { let mut inner = self . inner . lock () . unwrap () ; let channel_id = inner . channel_id . as_channel_id () ; let values : Vec < V > = inner . buf . drain (..) . collect () ; let count = values . len () ; for value in values { let record = ReclamationRecord :: for_channel_close (scope_id , sweep_epoch , hash_of (& value) , channel_id ,) ; sink . emit (record) ; } inner . closed = true ; count } }
